title: Saxifraga engleri
taxonomy: Saxifraga engleri is classified as a species within the Saxifraga genus, which belongs to the Saxifragaceae family. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as saxifrages or rockfoils. The Saxifraga genus is native to the Northern Hemisphere, and Saxifraga engleri is one of the many species within it. Saxifraga engleri is a plant species that is native to the Himalayan region. It is often found growing in alpine and subalpine meadows, rocky areas, and mountain slopes.

intro: Saxifraga engleri, also known as Chinese saxifrage, is a type of flowering plant in the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential uses in treating respiratory and cardiovascular conditions. Bioactive constituents of Saxifraga engleri include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Saxifraga engleri is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are marketed to promote respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
